On the icy shores of the North Sea, a "graveyard" under construction is raising the hopes of climate experts: soon, the site will house a small portion of the CO2 emitted by European industry, thus preventing it from ending up in the atmosphere.
For a long time perceived as a technically complicated and costly solution of marginal utility, carbon capture and storage (CCS) is now in vogue on a planet that is struggling to reduce its emissions despite the climate emergency. The cooperative movement was once at the forefront of the global struggle for consumer rights, affordable rents, and even... world peace.
The words "cooperative" and "partnership" are literally everywhere in everyday life in Switzerland. Its content, however, is very different from that of the word "cooperative" in Russian. Cooperative apartment? This is probably the most common concept that arises in the mind of a post-Soviet person when saying this word. 3072.text_600:
Academics, civil engineers and architects from three of six funds worth £23bn vote to sell off coal and high risk oil and gas – but lawyers, vets and other engineers vote against divestment

Academics, civil engineers and architects in Denmark have voted in favour of their pension funds selling off coal and high risk oil and gas investments, because of the role of fossil fuels in driving climate change.

However, votes on divestment by lawyers, vets and other engineers were narrowly lost.
